title: Fix search page: broken /domains link + hardcoded 350 count + missing aria-label
description:
There are THREE issues to fix in this file:

### Issue 1: Broken link to /domains (404)
In the no-results empty state, there's a link 'Browse by Domain' pointing to '/domains' which returns 404. There is no /domains page. Change href="/domains" to href="/catalog" since the catalog page has domain filtering.

### Issue 2: Hardcoded 'over 350' paper count
The subtitle on line 16 says 'Explore over 350 papers, controversies, and glossary terms.' The 350 is hardcoded. The client-side JS updates this dynamically but the initial server-rendered HTML is wrong. Fix: import catalog data in the frontmatter (add `import catalog from '../data/catalog.json';` and `const totalPapers = catalog.papers.length;`), then change the subtitle text to use the dynamic count: `Explore ${totalPapers} papers, controversies, and glossary terms.`

### Issue 3: Main search input lacks aria-label
The input with id='search-input' has no aria-label and no associated <label>. Screen readers can't identify it. Add aria-label="Search papers by title, author, or abstract" to the input element.

Do NOT output any thinking or explanation. Output ONLY search/replace blocks.

title: Fix homepage: skeptical count uses wrong field + remove allPaperIds bloat
description:
There are TWO issues to fix in this file:

### Issue 1: Skeptical count inconsistency
The homepage computes skepticalCount using `p.folder === '10_Skeptical_and_Critical'` which yields 49, but the stat is labeled 'Critical Views (Skeptical Analysis papers)'. The Research Domains section below shows 'Skeptical Analysis — 28 papers' (from psi_domain === 'skeptical'). These disagree.

Fix: Change the label from '(Skeptical Analysis papers)' to '(across all domains)' to clarify the 49 includes critical papers from multiple domains, not just the skeptical domain. This is more honest than changing the count.

### Issue 2: 10KB of paper IDs inlined into homepage HTML
Line 63 creates `const allPaperIds = papers.map((p: any) => p.id)` which inlines all 354 paper ID strings (~10KB) into the HTML. This is only used by the 'Surprise Me' button. There's already a `randomPaperIds` array (line 60) with 20 pre-selected IDs.

Fix:
1. Remove the line `const allPaperIds = papers.map((p: any) => p.id);`
2. Find the `define:vars` that passes both randomPaperIds and allPaperIds, and remove allPaperIds from it
3. In the Surprise Me button JS, change any reference to `allPaperIds` to use `randomPaperIds` instead

Do NOT output any thinking or explanation. Output ONLY search/replace blocks.
